summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on Case No. BZA-2023-006, a variance request to expand a nonconforming commercial structure in a residential zoning district by adding 1,792 square feet for cold storage of cars and auto parts restoration. The Board discussed front and rear yard setback reductions, dumpster placement, landscaping requirements, and propane tank relocation. Neighboring property owners spoke in support. The Board unanimously approved the variance with staff-recommended conditions.
decisions: Motion to amend agenda to add Chairperson's statement and swearing in of witnesses passed unanimously
Motion to approve video minutes from February 27 meeting passed unanimously

Motion to approve variance BZA-2023-006 (expansion of nonconforming structure, front yard setback reduction from 50 ft to 8.76 ft, rear yard setback reduction from 50 ft to 21.24 ft) with staff conditions passed unanimously
Motion to move June BZA meeting from June 19 to June 20, 2023 at 6:30 PM passed unanimously
action_items: Applicant (Luke Perry) to submit a landscape plan to township planning staff for review and approval, including screening/landscaping at rear of building addition
Applicant to submit dumpster enclosure plan for staff-level review and approval, likely positioned at rear corner of new addition
Applicant to remove or relocate propane tanks underground
Applicant to remove the two mobile storage containers from the property
Applicant to confirm dumpster enclosure materials are consistent with building materials and meet code requirements
Staff (Liz) to interview an alternate BZA member at the next trustee meeting on May 23
key_amounts: Front yard setback reduction from 50 feet to 8.76 feet (lowest lane)
Rear yard setback reduction from 50 feet to 21.24 feet
5 foot accessory structure setback from property line for structures under 200 sq ft
20 foot side yard setback requirement in R3 district
notable_topics: Variance request BZA-2023-006 for expansion of nonconforming commercial building in R3 residential district
Front and rear yard setback reductions for building addition
Propane tank removal or underground relocation
Landscaping and screening requirements for residential neighbors
Rescheduling June BZA meeting from June 19 to June 20
notes: This was the BZA's first meeting using a new staff report format with staff recommendations broken down by review criteria. Board members expressed appreciation for the improved format. The property is located at the corner of State Route 48 and Lowest Lane, south of State Route 28. The meeting opened at 6:30 PM and adjourned at 7:00 PM. Despite the video title referencing BZA, the transcript initially refers to it as a Board of Trustees meeting.
title: BZA 5-15-2023
